title: rubus rhamnifolius subsp. obtusangulus
intro: Rubus rhamnifolius subsp. obtusangulus, also known as the obtusangulus raspberry or wild raspberry, is a type of bramble that belongs to the Rubus genus, characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruits. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rubus rhamnifolius subsp. obtusangulus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against certain diseases, such as cancer and neurodegenerative disorders. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, stems, and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
